Error: Cannot call impure function during render

`Date.now` is an impure function. Calling an impure function can produce unstable results that update unpredictably when the component happens to re-render. (https://react.dev/reference/rules/components-and-hooks-must-be-pure#components-and-hooks-must-be-idempotent).

C:\Users\andre\Desktop\LICITANEST\src\componentes\contratos\ContratoAtivoCard.tsx:34:58

> 34 |     ? Math.ceil((new Date(contrato.data_fim).getTime() - Date.now()) / 86400000)
     |                                                          ^^^^^^^^^^ Cannot call impure function
  35 |     : null;
  36 |
  37 |   const pctVigencia = contrato?.data_inicio && contrato?.data_fim

Error: Cannot call impure function during render

`Date.now` is an impure function. Calling an impure function can produce unstable results that update unpredictably when the component happens to re-render. (https://react.dev/reference/rules/components-and-hooks-must-be-pure#components-and-hooks-must-be-idempotent).

C:\Users\andre\Desktop\LICITANEST\src\componentes\contratos\ContratoAtivoCard.tsx:39:11

  38 |     ? Math.min(100, Math.max(0, Math.round(
> 39 |         ((Date.now() - new Date(contrato.data_inicio).getTime()) /
     |           ^^^^^^^^^^ Cannot call impure function
  40 |         (new Date(contrato.data_fim).getTime() - new Date(contrato.data_inicio).getTime())) * 100
  41 |       )))
  42 |     : 0;

Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

C:\Users\andre\Desktop\LICITANEST\src\componentes\layout\SuperAdminLayout.tsx:267:5
  265 |   // Fechar menu mobile ao navegar
  266 |   useEffect(() => {
> 267 |     setMobileMenuOpen(false);
      |     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  268 |   }, [location.pathname]);
  269 |
  270 |   const iniciais = servidor

messages.message:
Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

C:\Users\andre\Desktop\LICITANEST\src\paginas\superadmin\ContratosPage.tsx:179:21
  177 |   }, [page, statusFiltro, filtroPrefeitura, busca, sortKey, sortDir]);
  178 |
> 179 |   useEffect(() => { fetchResumo(); }, [fetchResumo]);
      |                     ^^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  180 |   useEffect(() => { fetchContratos(); }, [fetchContratos]);
  181 |
  182 |   // Sync search params

Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

C:\Users\andre\Desktop\LICITANEST\src\paginas\superadmin\ContratosPage.tsx:180:21
  178 |
  179 |   useEffect(() => { fetchResumo(); }, [fetchResumo]);
> 180 |   useEffect(() => { fetchContratos(); }, [fetchContratos]);
      |                     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  181 |
  182 |   // Sync search params
  183 |   useEffect(() => {

Error: Cannot create components during render

Components created during render will reset their state each time they are created. Declare components outside of render.

C:\Users\andre\Desktop\LICITANEST\src\paginas\superadmin\ContratosPage.tsx:392:34
  390 |                 <th className="px-4 py-3 text-left font-medium">
  391 |                   <button type="button" onClick={() => toggleSort("numero_contrato")} className="flex items-center gap-1">
> 392 |                     N┬║ Contrato <SortIcon col="numero_contrato" />
      |                                  ^^^^^^^^ This component is created during render
  393 |                   </button>
  394 |                 </th>
  395 |                 <th className="px-4 py-3 text-left font-medium">

C:\Users\andre\Desktop\LICITANEST\src\paginas\superadmin\ContratosPage.tsx:218:3

  217 |
> 218 |   function SortIcon({ col }: { col: SortKey }) {
      |   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 219 |     if (sortKey !== col) return <ChevronUp className="h-3 w-3 opacity-20" />;
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 220 |     return sortDir === "asc" ? <ChevronUp className="h-3 w-3" /> : <ChevronDown className="h-3 w-3" />;
      | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
> 221 |   }

Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).
